description: MilPack school packing list data — edit these JSON files to update the app without code changes.

schools.name: Basic Combat Training
schools.description: 10-week foundational Army training for all enlisted soldiers. Covers basic combat skills, physical fitness, weapons qualification, and Army values.

schools.name: Navy Boot Camp
schools.description: 7-week Recruit Training Command at Great Lakes, IL. The Navy's only boot camp, covering seamanship, firefighting, first aid, and military customs.

schools.name: Marine Corps Boot Camp
schools.location: Parris Island, SC / San Diego, CA
schools.description: 13-week Marine Corps Recruit Training at Parris Island, SC or San Diego, CA. The longest and most physically demanding of the service boot camps.

schools.name: Air Force Basic Training
schools.description: 8.5-week Basic Military Training at Lackland Air Force Base (Joint Base San Antonio). Covers Airman standards, physical fitness, and Air Force core values.

schools.name: Air Assault School
schools.description: 10-day course teaching sling load operations, rappelling, and pathfinder techniques. Known as the 'Ten Toughest Days in the Army.'

schools.name: Ranger School
schools.description: 61-day Army leadership school focused on small unit tactics. Three phases: Benning, Mountain, and Florida. One of the most demanding courses in the military.

schools.name: SF Assessment & Selection
schools.description: 24-day assessment and selection process for Special Forces candidates. Evaluates physical endurance, land navigation, teamwork, and psychological resilience.

schools.name: Navy SEAL BUD/S
schools.location: Naval Special Warfare Center, Coronado, CA
schools.description: Basic Underwater Demolition/SEAL training. 24-week pipeline including Hell Week, combat diving, and land warfare. The most elite selection course in the U.S. military.

schools.name: MARSOC Assessment & Selection
schools.description: 3-week Marine Raider assessment and selection at Camp Lejeune, NC. Evaluates physical endurance, land navigation, teamwork, and leadership potential for the Marine Corps' special operations forces.

schools.name: PSYOP Basic Qualification Course
schools.description: Approximately 20-week course at Fort Liberty, NC training soldiers in psychological operations planning, media production, and target audience analysis. Prepares candidates for assignment to a PSYOP unit.

schools.name: Civil Affairs Assessment & Selection
schools.description: Approximately 3-week assessment and selection course at Fort Liberty, NC evaluating candidates for Civil Affairs. Tests physical fitness, problem-solving, communication skills, and cultural awareness.

schools.name: Special Forces Small Unit Tactics
schools.description: The Small Unit Tactics (SUT) phase of the Special Forces Qualification Course (SFQC) at Camp Mackall, Fort Liberty, NC. Approximately 3 weeks of intense field training in small unit tactics, land navigation, and combat operations for Special Forces candidates.

schools.name: ARSOF Basic Leader Course
schools.description: The Army Special Operations Forces Basic Leader Course (ARSOF BLC) at Camp Mackall, Fort Liberty, NC. The ARSOF-specific version of the Army's Basic Leader Course, preparing NCOs across Special Forces, PSYOP, and Civil Affairs for leadership roles in special operations units.
